Transcrição Completa: How To Improve Your Social Presence

 

 

 

[00:00]"You heard me say it in the last video on how to gain expert positioning. You got to clean up your social profiles and you've got to go get some professional photos taken of you."

 

 

 

[00:10]"And I mean it when I say there are people around you who are absolutely calling themselves photographers that you probably went to school with or just in your local area probably charging $25 to $100 tops for for tens of photos."

 

 

 

[00:22]"I mean, you probably get good 30, 50 photos taken by one of these local photographers who's got a nice DSLR camera, knows how to touch them up in Photoshop a little bit. That can really help turn your social media profiles around."

 

 

 

[00:35]"I've had students come into the program, and I'm not talking to everybody here, but I'm talking to a majority for sure. Some of you guys don't even have profile pictures yet."

 

 

 

[00:44]"You know, you join into the group or you follow me on Instagram, and like literally you just you don't even have a profile photo. You have the gray icon or maybe you have two to three, maybe 10 photos."

 

 

 

[00:56]"You are transitioning into becoming an agency owner or depending on who you are in this program, you're scaling your agency. You got to understand it doesn't take an experience for you to shift your identity."

 

 

 

[01:08]"But it does take some key self-awareness from you to understand that when you choose to start playing the role of a professional, an agency owner, and an expert, it will require you to take your social media and to make it professional."

 

 

 

[01:22]"If you have a screen name like Smoking Weeds98, you got to change your screen name. Go get a new profile, right? You can use Instagram for personal reasons on that one. Get a professional uh username, right?"

 

 

 

[01:35]"If you're in that situation, there's a website you can use called brandyourself.com. Okay, brandyourself.com. I heard this on Shark Tank a while ago, so a few years ago, I heard of this service."

 

 

 

[01:47]"I I don't even know if they got funded or not, but it's been something I've paid for every month, no matter what, where you can go connect your social media accounts to it and it'll automatically scan through all of the history of every single thing you've ever posted, commented, and it'll flag keywords."

 

 

 

[02:04]"There were things when I was 16, 17 years old, I would say that were just fucking stupid that a kid would say, right, that I wanted to go delete because I am a professional and that's not who I am. It's not how I represent myself."

 

 

 

[02:17]"So, that's my point. You need to go do a cleanup of everything that's already happened in the past. Most likely Twitter more than anything else, Facebook back in the day, depending on how old you are and how you were interacting on the platform."

 

 

 

[02:30]"Of course, Instagram, whatever photos and videos you guys have. There are there are a lot of younger students inside this program. So, to be very direct with the younger crowd, if you're going through college, you're going through high school."

 

 

 

[02:42]"I take myself back when I was in your shoes and I say to myself, you know, I remember thinking it was cool to like post a photo that I would party or like to post a photo with friends at a concert or something or to, you know, just go out of my way and make some funny photos that I would put out there on the internet."

 

 

 

[03:00]"They're just unprofessional and they do nothing for you when you're making money with the internet rather than just participating on the internet."

 

 

 

[03:08]"Delete all those shitty photos you have of yourself where you're incoherent or when you're not representing yourself how you'd want to represent yourself."

 

 

 

[03:16]"Now, go ahead and take some professional photos. Like I said, local photographers is very cheap. Have your mom do it. Have your cousin do it. Have your friend do it. Do it on the iPhone in portrait mode."

 

 

 

[03:27]"You know, change outfits. Clean yourself up. Go get a haircut. Uh go buy some nicer clothes. Make yourself feel good and empower yourself with a new identity that you're going to evolve into. Right?"

 

 

 

[03:38]"Write down your 2.0 self and the characteristics that come with that. And build yourself into the next person you visually want to be and represent yourself the right way on social media."

 

 

 

[03:49]"Start with a cleanup. Leverage brandyourself.com if you want to have a tool that automatically goes back for you and helps you with the cleanup. In addition to I have no affiliation with brand yourself, by the way."

 

 

 

[04:02]"In addition to that, make sure you go get some photos taken and start dropping them out there. See you in the next video."

 

 

 

 

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

 

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

$25 a $100 - Custo de fotógrafos locais

30 a 50 fotos - Quantidade média de fotos profissionais que você pode obter

2 a 3 fotos - Número insuficiente de fotos que muitos têm no perfil

16-17 anos - Idade mencionada de postagens antigas problemáticas

 

 

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Limpeza de Perfis Sociais:

Remover fotos não profissionais (festas, shows, momentos incoerentes)

Deletar comentários e posts antigos inadequados

Mudar usernames não profissionais (ex: "SmokingWeeds98")

Usar brandyourself.com para scan automático de histórico

 

Fotografia Profissional Acessível:

Contratar fotógrafos locais ($25-$100)

Usar iPhone em modo retrato

Pedir ajuda de familiares/amigos com câmeras DSLR

Fazer múltiplas trocas de roupa

 

Transformação de Identidade:

Escrever características do "2.0 self"

Fazer corte de cabelo

Comprar roupas mais profissionais

Separar perfis pessoais de profissionais

 

 

 

ESTRUTURA:

Transição de Identidade: De participante da internet para profissional que ganha dinheiro com internet

Expert Positioning: Representação visual alinhada com autoridade profissional

Social Media Cleanup: Processo sistemático de auditoria e remoção de conteúdo não profissional

Plataformas Prioritárias: Twitter, Facebook, Instagram (mencionadas como principais para limpeza)

 

 

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Investimento em Fotos: $25-$100 (uma única vez)

Brandyourself.com: Serviço de assinatura mensal (valor não especificado)

ROI: Transição de identidade visual para suportar crescimento de agência

 

 

 

FERRAMENTA RECOMENDADA:

brandyourself.com - Plataforma de gerenciamento de reputação online

Conecta contas de redes sociais

Scan automático de histórico completo

Identifica palavras-chave problemáticas

Facilita limpeza de posts/comentários antigos

Origem: Shark Tank

Sem afiliação do instrutor

 

 

 

DURAÇÃO: ~4 minutos FORMATO: Vídeo educacional direto ao ponto / Mentoria para donos de agência PÚBLICO-ALVO: Estudantes do programa, jovens empreendedores, donos de agência em crescimento
